message:
Fix URL double-escaping and add UTF-8 safety in html_export renderer

Bug fix:
- render_links(): Remove redundant html_escape() call that was causing
  double-escaping of ampersands in URLs with query parameters.
  Before: https://example.com?a=1&b=2 → &amp;b=2 (broken)
  After: https://example.com?a=1&b=2 → &b=2 (correct)

UTF-8 safety improvements:
- render_message(): Use truncate_to_char_boundary() for message preview
- render_tool_call(): Safe truncation of long tool outputs
- format_timestamp(): Safe truncation of timestamps with unicode
- Add truncate_to_char_boundary() utility function

Tests added:
- test_url_with_query_params_not_double_escaped
- test_truncate_to_char_boundary
- test_long_message_collapse_utf8_safe
- test_tool_output_truncation_utf8_safe
- test_format_timestamp_utf8_safe

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
